title: Understanding Angular Velocity
text: Angular velocity is the rate of change of angular displacement and is usually measured in radians per second.

title: Formula for Angular Velocity
text: The formula for angular velocity (ω) is given by $$ω=\frac{\theta}{t}$$, where θ is the angular displacement in radians and $$t$$ is the time in seconds.

title: Finding Angular Displacement
text: Since the track is circular, the angular displacement θ can be found using the formula $$\theta=\frac{s}{r}$$, where s is the arc length (400 m) and $$r$$ is the radius of the track.

title: Calculate the Radius
text: Assuming the track is a perfect circle, calculate the radius using the circumference formula $$C=2pir$$, where C is the circumference (400 m).

title: Calculate Angular Displacement
text: Using the radius calculated, find the angular displacement $$\theta=\frac{s}{r}$$.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Now, use the formula $$ω=\frac{\theta}{t}$$ to find the angular velocity.
